issue_body:
The `dim` modifier does not handle being nested inside another style correctly. When nesting `dim` inside `bold`, the dim style bleeds out and affects the surrounding styled text.

Reproduction:

```js
const chalk = require('chalk');
console.log(chalk.bold(chalk.dim('bold-dim'), 'bold'));
```

Expected: The word 'bold' after the dim section should appear in bold only (not dimmed).
Actual: The word 'bold' also appears dimmed, because the closing sequence for `dim` removes the parent `bold` style without re-opening it.

This occurs because `bold` and `dim` share the same ANSI closing sequence (`\e[22m`). When the inner style is closed, its closing sequence also terminates the parent style. The parent style's opening sequence is not re-emitted after the inner style ends, so subsequent text loses the parent formatting.

The bug is reproducible in Terminal.app and iTerm on macOS.

More generally, any nested styles that share closing ANSI codes (such as bold + dim, or multiple color nesting) will exhibit this same bleeding behavior where the inner style is not properly closed before the parent style resumes.
